title: Lesson 227
transcript: Let's finish talking about business continuity management by talking about what are the primary goals of business continuity management. And as a reminder, business continuity management is the overall business process of making sure that we create our DRP, and BCP plans and we test them and we have people that are trained and know what they're doing and all those sorts of things. So what are the overall most important goals of business continuity management? Number one, and I'll give you a giant hint here. It's the same number one goal of physical security. The number one goal of business continuity management is safety of people. And this is ultimately what is most critical to do in the event of a disaster because as we recall a disaster is a major unexpected massive damaging event to the organization and so the very most important thing to protect is people People are the most valuable resources, assets of any organization. And without people, you can't do any sort of recovery. So the number one goal of business continuity management, safety of people. Next most important goal is to try and minimize the damage. So whatever major disaster is occurring, figuring out how you minimize that damage. Those are the two most important critical things to focus on as soon as the disaster occurs. And then a little bit longer term is figuring out how do you ensure the survival of the business. So those are the three goals of business continuity management. And one final reminder, what does business continuity management focus on? Well, only the most critical and essential functions or services within a business.
